experiment_section: Anchoring Study A in Motivated Errors

instructions.description: Overview: Explains the experiment structure — two parts, understanding questions, part-that-counts payment mechanism.
instructions.html_content: Overview : Throughout this study, you will be provided with instructions.  Please carefully read all instructions.  Sometimes, you will be asked understanding questions about the instructions to make sure you understand them before proceeding.

instructions.description: Payment overview: Explains the two-part structure, show-up fee, and that one part will be randomly selected as the part-that-counts.
instructions.html_content: Your Payment: This study involves two parts — Part 1 and Part 2 — followed by a short survey. For completing this study, you will receive a minimum payment of ${e://Field/showup}

instructions.description: Part 1 Instructions: Explains the list-based decision task in Part 1 — each row presents two payment options, participant switches from left to right at their preferred row.
instructions.html_content: Part 1 Instructions   In Part 1, you will have to make several decisions by completing one list.  Each row of the list will present two payment options.   - The payment option on the left...

instructions.description: Part 2 Instructions: Explains that participants will guess answers to trivia questions, that each question is preceded by an anchor comparison question, and that they have 15 seconds per main question.
instructions.html_content: Instructions for Part 2:   In Part 2, you will be asked to guess the answer to ${e://Field/main_decisions} main questions. Prior to answering each main question, you will first be asked...

instructions.description: Part 2 payment rule: Explains that if Part 2 is the part-that-counts, one main question is randomly selected and the payment allocated in that question is distributed.
instructions.html_content: If Part 2 is randomly selected as the part-that-counts, one main question from this part will be randomly selected. Then, the payment that is allocated in that question will be distributed.&

instructions.description: Treatment instruction (Overestimate / too-high incentive): If guess is too high (≥3 above actual), charity gets stake cents; describes the payment rule for the overestimate treatment.
instructions.html_content: If your guess is too high  because it is 3 or more above the actual number, ${e://Field/charityA_name} will be allocated a donation of ${e://Field/stake} cents .  If your

instructions.description: Treatment instruction (Underestimate / too-low incentive for charity): If guess is too low (≥3 below actual), charity gets stake cents; describes the payment rule for the underestimate treatment.
instructions.html_content: If your guess is too low  because it is 3 or more below the actual number, ${e://Field/charityA_name} will be allocated a donation of ${e://Field/stake} cents.  If your g

instructions.description: Treatment instruction (alternative too-low incentive variant): If guess is too low (≥3 below actual), charity gets stake cents; variant of the underestimate payment rule screen.
instructions.html_content: If your guess is too low  because it is 3 or more below the actual number, ${e://Field/charityA_name} will be allocated a donation of ${e://Field/stake} cents .  If your

instructions.description: Comprehension check passed confirmation: Tells participant they answered the understanding question correctly and reminds them they have 15 seconds per main question in Part 2.
instructions.html_content: That's correct!  Please proceed to read begin answering the main questions in Part 2.  Recall that you will only have 15 seconds to answer each main question.

screens.description: Question 1 (low anchor = 20): Anchor comparison — is light travel time from Sun to Jupiter more or less than 20 minutes? Then slider guess on 0–100 scale.

parameters.question_topic: Minutes for light to travel from Sun to Jupiter

screens.description: Question 1 slider response (low anchor): Participant moves slider (0–100, default=20) to enter their guess for light travel time Sun to Jupiter.
Question 2 (low anchor = 20): Anchor comparison — did the first airplane trip across the US take more or less than 20 days? Then slider guess on 0–100 scale.

parameters.question_topic: Days for first airplane trip across the continental US (Calbraith Perry Rodgers, 1911)

screens.description: Question 2 slider response (low anchor): Participant moves slider (0–100, default=20) to enter their guess for days of first US airplane trip.
Question 3 (low anchor = 20): Anchor comparison — is the population of Uzbekistan (2018) more or less than 20 million? Then slider guess on 0–100 scale.

parameters.question_topic: Population of Uzbekistan in millions (as of 2018)

screens.description: Question 3 slider response (low anchor): Participant moves slider (0–100, default=20) to enter their guess for Uzbekistan population in millions.
Question 4 (low anchor = 20): Anchor comparison — is the weight of the Eiffel Tower's metal structure more or less than 20 hundred tons? Then slider guess on 0–100 scale.

parameters.question_topic: Weight of the Eiffel Tower metal structure in hundreds of tons

screens.description: Question 4 slider response (low anchor): Participant moves slider (0–100, default=20) to enter their guess for Eiffel Tower weight in hundreds of tons.
